Stand in line with everybody else since the wrong message. When someone comes to my house for a consulting day, my Ohio house, and there I have all kinds of stuff. Here, they rent a suite at the Hyatt that's a walking distance from my condo and we meet in the hotel. But at my Ohio house, I have all my stuff there because it's where I work, right? Well, I can, of course, prepare if I know that Charlie Martin's coming for a day of consulting. I know kind of what we're going to talk about and I know what might be interesting for him to see. And I could go round it all up ahead of time and have it in the conference room. That doesn't have near the impact of me going and hunting something down for him while he is there and dredging it out of archives and files, killing the deer and dragging it back into the room for him while he's there. That has much more impact. Consequently, I don't go find this stuff ahead of time. It has more impact if I go hunt for it while he's there waiting for me to hunt for it. It has the added benefit of not requiring preparation for consulting days. But setting that aside, it's just like one thing may have a time management benefit that also has a positioning and a posturing reason. This, too, has a practical benefit. I don't have to prepare, right? And if I prepare, I'm going to round up eight or ten things and maybe he really only winds up seeing three of them, so now I get a bunch of preparation for nothing. This way I'm using his time that he's paying for to do the hunt, not my time on the day before, that I'm paying for to do the hunt and he likes to hunt. Remember, the job of the performance is to delight the audience. So he's more delighted because he saw me go into the back room and he hears drawers being slammed and I'm in there killing the beast and oh, look, I found it for you. Didn't know if I was going to be able to get it, but here it is, right? Well, they like that better. So why not do it? It's part of the performance, right? And sometimes I know where it is, but I go to the back room and I stand there and I count thousand, one thousand, two thousand, three thousand, you know, and then I come out with what it is that I found. Now, again, as I was preparing for this and thinking through these things, I mean, I do that now. This is the first time I've consciously thought about it and therefore enunciated it in a long time. I do it unconsciously by rote and it's been so long since I had a conscious thought about it. I can't even tell you when I didn't do it, when I started doing it. I don't know any of that anymore, but I do know that there's a different impact from both ways. I have neatly organized everything ahead of time and people aren't impressed, but they are really impressed if they see me go find it. So better they should see me go find it, right? It's the same thing as if I could write them a headline right then and there and I do it. That's not as impressive as if they think I agonized after they left and lost sleep over this and then faxed it to them the next day while they're more impressed. It's the same three minutes. It's just that they didn't see the three minutes. Now, if the three minutes was like really impressive, you know, if like I waved my hand and the headline came out of the hand, well then I'd want to do it in front of them, right? Because that's pretty cool. But just seeing me think of it, write it on a piece of paper, that ain't real cool, right? So now the mental picture they have of, oh my God, he's sweated all night over this and he dug around in his files and he found this and now he sent it to me. In fact, he has more impact, right? It has even more impact if they wait three or four days to get it. It leads us to 40. This is a real important statement. You cannot abruptly or occasionally exercise control, right? Like you can't have no bedtime with the kids for months and then because Aunt Jane's standover, try and impose bedtime. No good. Lots of luck. So we either have control or we don't have control. We can't not have control and occasionally get it in times when we really need it or want it. So you sort of build control continuously from the beginning all the way through the relationship. So in selling, right, we exercise control in many cases by when we put the paperwork in front of them or how we have part of it filled out ahead of time for them.
